title: Clank & Slop — The Tape
tagline: — READ THE TAPE · ALL THE SLOP THAT'S FIT TO PRICE —

props.label: At a Glance · From the Tape Desk

bullets.text: Brent fell to about $79.61, heading for a roughly 9% weekly fall as supply began moving through Hormuz — but the shipping system is treating the Strait as a mined, permissioned corridor, not an open lane: ~12–15 transits a day vs 120–140 pre-war, ~550 ships queued, the central route still closed under ~80 mines.

The first Warsh Fed held at 3.50%–3.75% unanimously but moved the regime: the dot plot tilted to one 2026 hike (nine of 19), guidance was stripped back, and traders now price a better-than-90% chance of an October hike. Oil eases inflation while the Fed leans the other way.

SpaceX is running the float as machinery: $85.7B raised after the greenshoe, then SPCX off more than 6% on a small public float, while the $60.0B Cursor deal is priced off SPCX's VWAP — a higher tape means less dilution. A bond of at least $20B is the second gear.

JPMorgan pulled Claude for Hong Kong staff after Goldman — the export order propagating into vendor licensing and bank compliance, turning a frontier API into a policy endpoint that can be switched off by state order, contract reading or an approved-tool list.

props.title: How permissioned the Strait still is
props.subtitle: Hormuz transit reality · vessels per day
props.source: Reuters (12–15/day in recent weeks; 120–140 pre-war), AP (central route mined, shoulder routes in use)

items.why: Postponed over the Lebanon fighting; whether a new date reappears tests Foreman's procedural read against the escalation desk's missing-party read.
Reuters flags 40–50 days before many insurers and owners are confident enough to sail; the desk watches transits, JWC war-risk listing and insurance, not the communiqué.

items.what: Hormuz toll-free window · nuclear, Lebanon, enforcement
items.why: The fee waiver is suspended pricing, not a concession — the hard files are deferred to the negotiation period.
With guidance stripped back, each inflation, wage and energy print now carries more rate volatility than under explicit guidance.
Whether regulators read it as an application-layer tuck-in or AI-infrastructure concentration attached to orbital networks — with $4B and $10B fees around failure.

items.what: Iran remains on the latest published Joint War Committee risk list, so a lower Brent tape does not automatically strip out war-risk clauses, underwriter caution or charter-party friction — the insurance market is a separate price from crude.

Cybersecurity leaders at firms including Nvidia and Adobe urged the US to lift the Anthropic curbs, calling the restriction potentially "dangerous" — the counterpressure that switching off the most capable defensive models for the wrong users also degrades friendly testing and patching.

Over 500,000 SpaceX options traded in the first hour and more than a million by early afternoon — in a small-float stock, heavy derivatives turn price into a lever on the Cursor deal, not just a readout.
